title: hieracium murorum subsp. torticeps
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. torticeps, also known as Wall hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieracium murorum plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. Historically, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. The health benefits of Hieracium murorum subsp. torticeps are numerous, including its ability to stimulate the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections and promote overall well-being. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an infusion or tincture, and it can also be used as a topical application to treat skin conditions and wounds.
